rationale: The cited paper appears directly relevant to the citing paper’s core domain: both center on hedonic-versus-utilitarian trade-offs and reason/feeling-linked consumer choice mechanisms. The citing note says it “extends prior research on reason-versus-feelings trade-offs” from situational effects to stable individual differences, and the cited note is a prominent situational, justification-based account of those same trade-offs, so this is plausibly a substantive theoretical antecedent rather than an incidental mention.
